revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:磐船神社]] {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Iwafune Shrine | location = {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano City|1=Katano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kisaichi|ja|私市 (交野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaichi|1=Kisaichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126914635}} 9-chome 19-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|44|52|N|135|41|35.4|E|region:JP}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Sacred object = Heavenly Rock Ship (天の磐船) | Main festival = May 5 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Iwafune-jinja (Katano), haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Main Hall and Sacred Object "Heavenly Rock Ship" }} [[File:Iwafune-jinja (Katano), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Iwafune Shrine''' (磐船神社, Iwafune-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano City|1=Katano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Situated along the gorge of the {{ill|Amanogawa (river)|en|Amanogawa (river)|qid=Q11443656|lt=Amano River (Osaka)|1=Amanogawa (river)}}, it is known for its sacred object, the "Heavenly Rock Ship" (天の磐船), a boat-shaped megalith measuring approximately 12 meters in height and 12 meters in length, spanning the river. The shrine is also referred to as Iwafune Shrine (岩船神社) or Iwakoro Shrine (岩所神社). == deity == * Main deity: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The origins of the shrine are unclear, but according to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} descended to Mount Takeruga-mine in Kawachi Province aboard the "Heavenly Rock Ship." The shrine is considered the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} of the Katano clan, a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, which had significant influence in the region. After the Mononobe clan's defeat in the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|qid=Q534720|lt=Isshi Incident|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} by {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|qid=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}, the Katano branch of the clan was ousted, leading to the shrine's decline. Subsequently, residents of four nearby villages—Kisaichi, {{ill|Hoshida|ja|星田 (交野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshida|1=Hoshida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131983738}}, {{ill|Tahara|ja|田原 (四條畷市・生駒市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahara|qq=}}, and Minami-Tahara—took care of the shrine. During the medieval period, the shrine became a site for mountain worship and {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} practices. It thrived as a lodge for ascetic practitioners. The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Faith|ja|住吉信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1114013}}, centered on the deities {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, was introduced to the shrine due to connections between the Sumiyoshi Shrine priests (the Tsutsumi clan) and the descendants of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. Traces of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, such as stone statues of Buddhas and bodhisattvas, remain in the precincts. Repeated floods of the {{ill|Amanogawa (river)|en|Amanogawa (river)|qid=Q11443656|lt=Amano River (Osaka)|1=Amanogawa (river)}} caused the loss of shrine structures and artifacts over the years. In {{ill|1987|en|1987|qid=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} (Shōwa 62), two poems by {{ill|Banba Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banba Mitsuhei|1=Tomobayashi Mitsuhira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6537004}} were inscribed on stone tablets and dedicated to the shrine. One poem was his death verse, "Without a rudder, the ship cannot escape this world—thus, even Mount Iwafune is in vain," while the other was titled "Blessed Nation" and celebrated Japan as an unshakable rock. These were engraved by Sumiyoshi Shrine's chief priest and erected on a large stone in the precincts. == Precincts == * Sacred Object: "Heavenly Rock Ship" (天の磐船) - Said to have been used by {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to descend from heaven. Visitors can explore the surrounding caves, though solo visitors are prohibited for safety reasons. * Main Hall * {{ill|Iwafune Inari Shrine|ja|磐船稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwafune Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579293|1=Iwafune Inari Shrine}} * Shinkō Inari Shrine * Fudō Myōō Statue * {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato Shrine|ja|天の岩戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579294|1=Ama-no-Iwato Shrine}} - Aligned to face {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. * Four Shrine Myōjin (住吉四神): Statues of {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}, {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Avalokiteśvara|1=Avalokiteśvara}}, {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|qid=Q1465683|lt=Mahāsthāmaprāpta|1=Mahasthamaprapta}}, and {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha|1=Kṣitigarbha}} * Shrine Office == Nearby Sites == * White Dragon Waterfall - A sacred site for mountain ascetics and practitioners of {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}. == festivals == * Spring Festival: May 5 == Access == ; By Car * Located along the Iwafune Road (former {{ill|Japan National Route 168|en|Japan National Route 168|qid=Q2556495|lt=National Route 168 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 168}}). Parking available. ''' Approximately 12 km south of the intersection at National Route 1 and Amano River. ''' About 2 km north of the Kita-Tahara Bridge intersection on National Route 163. ; By Public Transport * From {{ill|Kintetsu Ikoma Station|ja|近鉄生駒駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Ikoma Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生駒駅|1=Ikoma Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5996117}}, take a Nara Kōtsū bus to the Kitatahara stop. Approximately a 10-minute walk from the stop. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Amanoiwato Shrine|en|Amanoiwato Shrine|qid=Q2841102|lt=Ama-no-Iwato Shrine|1=Amanoiwato Shrine}} * {{ill|Yata Kusu Tamahiko Shrine|ja|矢田坐久志玉比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Kusu Tamahiko Shrine|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584205}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwafune-jinja (Katano, Osaka)}} * [http://www.osk.3web.ne.jp/~iw082125/index-j.html Official Website of Iwafune Shrine] {{translated page|ja|磐船神社|version=41162519|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:磐船神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11587924}} [[Category:History of Katano, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
